qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JavaScript flatted parser exposes ambient Array objects and enables reviver-assisted prototype pollution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same JavaScript parse root cause as the advisory: attacker-controlled reference tokens are used as raw array property keys in `cjs/index.js` and `esm/index.js`, allowing `__proto__` to resolve to `Array.prototype` and leak a live prototype reference to the caller.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Go flatted parser panics on malformed out-of-bounds indices
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets a different implementation and root cause. This finding is about unchecked numeric indexing in `golang/pkg/flatted/flatted.go` causing a Go panic, not the advisory's JavaScript `parse()` prototype-chain lookup that exposes `Array.prototype` from `cjs/index.js` / `esm/index.js`.

selected_finding.title: JavaScript flatted parser exposes ambient Array objects and enables reviver-assisted prototype pollution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the exact advisory root cause in the JavaScript `parse()` implementation and the rerun PoC from `.judge` demonstrated end-to-end global `Array.prototype` pollution from an attacker-controlled `__proto__` reference token.
evidence: The advisory describes `parse()` using attacker-controlled string tokens as raw keys into the internal input array, specifically allowing `__proto__` to resolve through the array prototype chain to `Array.prototype`.
The selected finding cites the same vulnerable JavaScript locations (`repo/cjs/index.js:30`, `repo/esm/index.js:29`) and the same missing numeric-index validation on `input[value]`.

The copied PoC run log at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_ff8b5e7f/.judge/poc_runs/js_parse_reference_token_prototype_pollution/run.log` shows `[evohunt] resolved __proto__ -> Array.prototype`.

The same run log shows `[evohunt] observed Array.prototype.evohuntPolluted = evohunt-proof` and `[evohunt] SUCCESS: Array.prototype polluted via attacker-selected reference token`, which is the advisory's exploit consequence.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC parsed attacker-controlled reference tokens, resolved `__proto__` to `Array.prototype`, wrote `evohuntPolluted = evohunt-proof` through the parsed object path, and confirmed a fresh array exposed the polluted property.
